title: Walden
author: Henry David Thoreau
content: “I wanted to live deep and suck out all the marrow of life.”
action: Commit to maximum intensity in your current focus.

title: Leaves of Grass
author: Walt Whitman
content: “Do I contradict myself? Very well then I contradict myself, I am large, I contain multitudes.”
action: Accept the complexity and evolution of your own identity.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
content: “So we beat on, boats against the current, borne back ceaselessly into the past.”
action: Acknowledge the momentum of your own history.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
content: “It is not down in any map; true places never are.”
action: Trust your intuition when navigating uncharted territory.

title: A Farewell to Arms
author: Ernest Hemingway
content: “The world breaks everyone and afterward many are strong at the broken places.”
action: Use your past failures as structural reinforcement.
